sn1.71:1.1: At Sāvatthī. Standing to one side, that devata addressed the Blessed One in verse:
sn1.71:2.1: v.223"Having slain what does one sleep soundly?Having slain what does one not sorrow?What is the one thing, O Gotama,Whose killing you approve?"
sn1.71:3.1: v.224"Having slain anger, one sleeps soundly;Having slain anger, one does not sorrow;The killing of anger, O devata,With its poisoned root and honeyed tip:This is the killing the noble ones praise,For having slain that, one does not sorrow."
